How Should You Install Software on a Mac? Homebrew vs Official Download vs curl

If you've just started using a Mac for development, you've probably noticed there are several different ways to install the same tool — download a `.dmg` from the official site, install with Homebrew, run a `curl` install script, and tutorials sometimes recommend different approaches for the same tool. This post sorts through the common Mac install methods, pairs them with real examples (Git, Node.js, Python, VS Code), so you know which one to pick when.
Mac: Auto-Switch Node.js Version via nvm When Opening a Project

As a Node.js dev, switching Node.js versions comes up all the time. Each project uses a different version, and it's super easy to forget to switch. So here's a note on auto-switching with nvm whenever you open a project on Mac.
Fixing 'npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead'
After updating to Node.js 16.15.1 and npm 8.11.0, I kept seeing "npm WARN config global --global, --local are deprecated. Use --location=global instead". Here's a note on the fix.
nvm on Windows: Fixing "Access is denied" and "exit status 145"

Two common nvm errors on Windows — "nvm use exit status 1: Access is denied" and "exit status 145: The directory is not empty." Quick notes on both — the first is almost always a permissions issue (run as administrator), and the second is usually a leftover nodejs folder from a previous install that nvm can't overwrite.
Installing nvm on Mac OS X
As a frontend engineer who touches Node.js, nvm is a must-install. On Mac it's a bit more annoying than Windows — there's no dedicated installer.
